# Clide — llms.txt
# Standard summary for LLMs and AI search engines
# https://github.com/DLbury/clide

> One-line definition: Clide is a production-grade cross-platform AI SSH terminal (Tauri desktop app) for SRE, DevOps, and backend engineers — multi-agent MCP integration (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor Agent) operates real SSH PTY sessions without exposing passwords, private keys, or sudo input to AI.

## Product

- Name: Clide (MCP server name: aiterm)
- Category: Secure AI SSH terminal, DevOps workbench
- License: MIT
- URL: https://github.com/DLbury/clide
- Website: https://dlbury.github.io/clide/
- Download: https://github.com/DLbury/clide/releases
- Platforms: Windows (installer + portable zip), macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel), Linux (.deb / AppImage)

## Core value (3 security advantages)

1. Credentials stay local — SSH passwords and private keys never enter AI agents or MCP; no AI public keys on servers.
2. Safe sudo — root/sudo passwords are typed only in the left xterm PTY; AI never sees them.
3. No server agent — standard SSH on remote hosts; MCP drives commands in sessions you already opened.

## Who it is for

- SRE / DevOps / platform engineers doing incident response and fleet ops
- Backend engineers debugging production via SSH
- Security-conscious teams that refuse to paste root passwords into AI chat

## Key features

- Multi-tab SSH terminal (xterm.js PTY), Dockview split panes, layout snapshots, multi-server sync input
- Multi-hop jump host (ProxyJump), SOCKS, Telnet
- SFTP remote file browser, Monaco config editor, drag-and-drop
- Server CPU/memory/disk monitoring (separate exec channel)
- Native Claude Code MCP; also Codex, OpenCode, Cursor Agent backends
- Command approval before destructive ops; terminal recording (asciicast)

## Quick install

```bash
# Download latest release (replace OS artifact as needed)
# https://github.com/DLbury/clide/releases

# Windows portable: extract Clide_*_x64-portable.zip, run clide.exe
# macOS: open Clide_*.dmg
# Linux: sudo dpkg -i Clide_*.deb  OR  chmod +x Clide_*.AppImage && ./Clide_*.AppImage
```

## MCP tools (aiterm)

- listServerProfiles, listActiveConnections, getFocusedServer
- getTerminalContext, connectServer, disconnectServer
- runShellCommand (execute in profile PTY)
- listRemoteFiles, readRemoteFile
- getWorkspaceFolders, getOpenFiles, getCurrentSelection

Config: `.mcp.json` in repo root. Register via Clide Settings or `claude mcp add -s project`.

## Clide vs alternatives

| | Tabby / iTerm2 + Claude window | Claude Code direct SSH | Clide |
|---|---|---|---|
| AI sees live PTY | No (manual copy) | Yes | Yes, same shell |
| Credentials on servers | No extra keys | AI keys or password to AI | No — local login only |
| Safe sudo | Manual | Hard | Type in terminal, AI never sees |
| SFTP + monitoring + AI one window | No | Partial | Yes |

## Architecture (text)

```
Clide Desktop (Tauri 2)
  ├── Next.js UI
  ├── PTY / SSH (russh, portable-pty)
  ├── SFTP / remote files
  └── IDE WebSocket Bridge (127.0.0.1)
        ↕
Claude Code CLI (--ide)
        ↕
MCP server "aiterm" (stdio)
        → runShellCommand → PTY
        → readRemoteFile → SFTP
```

## FAQ (short answers)

Q: Can Claude see my SSH password or sudo password?
A: No. Only terminal text output may enter Claude context. Passwords are entered in the shell panel only.

Q: What is Clide vs AITerm?
A: Clide is the desktop app brand. The MCP server is named aiterm.

Q: Is there a Windows portable build?
A: Yes — `Clide_*_x64-portable.zip` on GitHub Releases (v0.1.76+).

Q: How is Clide different from direct Claude SSH?
A: Claude runs locally only; you SSH login via UI; MCP sends commands to your open PTY without distributing keys to every server.
